sourceMovie.title: As Above, So Below
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: As Above, So Below (2014) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. John Erick Dowdle's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 10 Cloverfield Lane
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If As Above, So Below clicked for you, 10 Cloverfield Lane (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after As Above, So Below?
faq.answer: Try 10 Cloverfield Lane, Afflicted, The Bourne Identity—each shares DNA with As Above, So Below while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is As Above, So Below?
faq.answer: As Above, So Below (2014) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is As Above, So Below part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "As Above, So Below Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
